mission.title: Install and Configure Linkerd on Kubernetes
mission.description: Linkerd is an ultralight, security-first service mesh for Kubernetes that enhances observability, reliability, and security for your microservices without requiring code changes. Installing Linkerd allows you to manage and monitor your microservices effectively.

steps.title: Add the Linkerd Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official Linkerd Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.
```bash
helm repo add linkerd https://cloudnativeapp.github.io/charts/curated
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Linkerd control plane
steps.description:
Install the Linkerd control plane using Helm, specifying the desired version and creating a namespace for Linkerd.
```bash
helm install linkerd linkerd/linkerd --version 0.4.1 --namespace linkerd --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Linkerd control plane pods are running successfully.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace linkerd
```

steps.title: Configure Linkerd with resource limits
steps.description:
Set resource limits for the Linkerd control plane components by editing the values.yaml file or using Helm to set them directly during installation.
```bash
helm upgrade linkerd linkerd/linkerd --namespace linkerd --set controller.resources.limits.cpu=500m --set controller.resources.limits.memory=512Mi
```

steps.title: Port-forward the Linkerd dashboard
steps.description:
Access the Linkerd dashboard by port-forwarding the service to your local machine.
```bash
kubectl port-forward -n linkerd svc/linkerd-dashboard 8084:8084
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Linkerd release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Linkerd release from your cluster.
```bash
helm uninstall linkerd --namespace linkerd
```

uninstall.title: Clean up Linkerd resources
uninstall.description:
Delete any remaining custom resource definitions (CRDs) related to Linkerd.
```bash
kubectl delete crd --all --namespace linkerd
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Linkerd namespace
uninstall.description:
Delete the Linkerd namespace to clean up all associated resources.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace linkerd
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up the current state of the Linkerd installation.
```bash
kubectl get all --namespace linkerd -o yaml > linkerd-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Ensure your Helm repository is up to date to get the latest charts.
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade Linkerd
upgrade.description:
Upgrade your Linkerd installation to the latest version.
```bash
helm upgrade linkerd linkerd/linkerd --namespace linkerd --version 0.4.1
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check that the Linkerd control plane pods are running the new version.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace linkerd
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If any Linkerd pods are in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs for the specific pod to diagnose the issue.
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace linkerd
```

troubleshooting.title: Helm installation fails
troubleshooting.description:
If the Helm installation fails, ensure that the Helm repository is correctly added and updated. Check for any network issues or permission errors.
```bash
helm repo list
```

troubleshooting.title: Dashboard not accessible
troubleshooting.description:
If you cannot access the Linkerd dashboard, ensure that the port-forward command is running and that you are using the correct port.
```bash
kubectl get svc --namespace linkerd
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Linkerd will have all control plane pods running in the 'linkerd' namespace, and you will be able to access the Linkerd dashboard through port-forwarding. Resource limits and configurations should be applied as needed.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add linkerd https://cloudnativeapp.github.io/charts/curated
helm repo update
helm upgrade linkerd linkerd/linkerd --namespace linkerd --set controller.resources.limits.cpu=500m --set controller.resources.limits.memory=512Mi

prerequisites.description: You need a Kubernetes cluster and the Helm and kubectl command-line tools installed.
